items.title: In-toto secures build pipelines
items.oneliner: Supply chain framework that signs every build step so teams can verify who ran what, when, and in order.

items.title: Topcoat simplifies Rust fullstack apps
items.oneliner: Rust web framework that renders HTML server-side and skips separate APIs by letting components fetch database data directly.

items.title: A modern Postgres rewrite begins
items.oneliner: Early Rust database project aiming for Postgres compatibility with modern internals, async execution, and easier extensibility.

items.title: Rust battery packs curate crates
items.oneliner: Prototype tool bundles recommended Rust crates for common tasks, giving teams a faster starting point than crates.io browsing.

items.title: Static search trees beat binary search
items.oneliner: Benchmarked data structure and code showing up to 40x higher query throughput than binary search on static integer sets.

items.title: SQLite tuning lessons from production
items.oneliner: Hands-on Django writeup showing how WAL, ANALYZE, and batched deletes fixed real SQLite performance and stability issues.

items.title: Host your own AIM server
items.oneliner: Practical Linux guide for running Open OSCAR Server, opening ports, and getting classic AIM clients online again.

items.title: Freya 0.4 rewrites Rust GUI
items.oneliner: Rust GUI library now uses typed builders instead of macros, improving type safety and making desktop UI code easier to extend.

items.title: A calculator for responsive CSS
items.oneliner: Interactive tool and walkthrough that generates responsive CSS from breakpoints without the usual trial and error math.

items.title: This tool flags LLM cliches
items.oneliner: Small writing utility that highlights repetitive AI phrases so docs, posts, and release notes sound less machine-generated.
